title: SSRF Defense, Prototype-Safe Data, Terminal Injection, Paths, and Resource Limits
description: Redirects escape the fixed host, parsed objects are merged into state, remote text contains ANSI or bidi controls, save paths are arbitrary, and response or command sizes are unbounded. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default TypeScript trust-boundary policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Revalidate scheme, hostname, port, path class, and redirect count for every destination and reject user-controlled egress.
Use own-property checks, explicit field construction, safe records, and no blind assignment from unknown objects.
Normalize or visibly escape control, ANSI, bidi, and non-printing sequences from commands, API payloads, and files.
